summary: Free-tier card on /pricing advertises usable features ("5 agent runs / month", "Resume tailoring + ATS scoring", "Community support") with CTA "Get started free", but a Free-plan account cannot execute a single agent run in production — the global subscription paywall (AETHER_REQUIRE_PAID_SUBSCRIPTION=true) requires an ACTIVE PAID subscription for ANY agent invocation, including the ones the Free card claims are included. Resume tailoring specifically (the paid-sounding half of the Free bullet "Resume tailoring + ATS scoring") is fully blocked; only ATS scoring (a separate, non-agent, non-paywalled endpoint) actually works for Free accounts.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (canonical login) — confirmed Free plan via GET /billing/entitlement: {"active_paid":false,"plan":{"id":"free","status":"active"},"requiresSubscription":true}.
2. Navigate to /pricing (authenticated). Observe the Free card lists '5 agent runs / month', 'Resume tailoring + ATS scoring', 'Community support' with no caveat.
3. POST /agents/tailor/run with a well-formed body {"job_id":"<any>"} using the admin bearer token.
4. Observe HTTP 402 {"detail":{"error":"subscription_required","message":"An active subscription is required to use Aether. Subscribe to unlock.","upgradeUrl":"/pricing"}}.
5. Repeat with POST /agents/scout/run and POST /agents/cover-letter/run — identical 402 subscription_required.

6. Navigate to /dashboard as the same Free admin account — the entire dashboard is replaced by a 'Subscribe to unlock Aether' paywall (data-testid=subscription-paywall), confirming zero agent features are reachable from the Free plan despite the /pricing card's claims.
7. VERIFY TWICE: repeated steps 3-4 in a completely fresh login session (new JWT) — same 402 subscription_required both times.
expected: A Free-tier plan card that lists specific included features and quota should let a Free account actually use those features (or the pricing page should honestly state the plan has zero usable agent capacity / is a placeholder for signup only).
observed: Free plan quota row exists (runsAllowed=5) but is entirely unusable — every agent endpoint 402s for any non-paid account, and the dashboard itself is paywalled with copy reading 'Aether is in limited beta. An active subscription is required to run the AI agents...' — directly contradicting the /pricing Free card's presentation.

summary: The pricing page markets a differentiated 'model tier' per plan (LIGHT/STANDARD/ADVANCED/PREMIUM MODEL TIER subtitle on each card, plus the Power-tier feature bullet 'Full model access'), but no code path in the backend connects a subscriber's plan to actual LLM model selection — every agent call resolves its model from a fixed task-type tier (REASONING/STRUCTURED/FAST via get_model()) that is identical for Free, Starter, Pro, and Power users. The Plan.modelTier DB column is read only for display on /pricing (apps/api/app/routers/billing.py:92,457) and is never referenced by any agent/service module.
reproduction: 1. Read GET /billing/plans response (test-artifacts/network-events-step1.json) — each plan carries a distinct modelTier string (light/standard/advanced/premium) rendered on /pricing as '<TIER> MODEL TIER'.

2. grep apps/api/app/agents/*.py and apps/api/app/services/resume_tailor.py for get_model( calls — all six call sites pass a fixed task-tier literal ("REASONING" or "STRUCTURED"), never a value derived from the user's plan or Plan.modelTier.
3. grep the entire apps/api/app tree for any code that reads plan['modelTier'] outside of the /billing/plans and /billing/subscription response-building code in billing.py — zero results.
4. Re-run both greps (verify twice) — identical, deterministic result both times.
expected: A paid tier claiming 'Advanced model tier' / 'Full model access' should route that plan's agent calls to a materially different (better) model than the Free tier's 'Light model tier'.
observed: Model selection is uniform across all plans for a given task type; the per-plan 'model tier' label is presentation-only copy with no backing implementation.

summary: No subscription-management, downgrade, or Stripe customer-portal affordance exists anywhere in the product UI. /pricing shows no 'current plan' indicator for authenticated users (identical render signed-in vs signed-out — test-artifacts/09-auth-pricing-load.png vs 01-unauth-load-full.png) and every paid CTA points only to checkout (upgrade), never to management. The frontend API client already defines openBillingPortal() and fetchSubscription() (apps/web/src/lib/api/billing.ts) and the backend POST /billing/portal endpoint works (confirmed 409 'No billing account yet' for the Free admin), but grep across the entire apps/web/src tree shows these two functions are imported/called by nothing except their own definition file and a unit test — no page or component wires them up. The dashboard paywall itself tells users 'You can still browse pricing and manage your account' (test-artifacts/20-dashboard-paywall-evidence-fresh-session2.png), but no such account-management UI exists.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123 (Free plan) and load /pricing — compare against the unauthenticated screenshot: no plan badge, no 'manage subscription' link, identical to the logged-out view.
2. grep -rln "openBillingPortal|fetchSubscription" apps/web/src --include=*.tsx | grep -v __tests__ → zero page/component results (only the definition file itself).

3. Load /dashboard as the Free admin — the paywall copy explicitly promises 'you can... manage your account' with a single CTA ('View plans & subscribe') that only leads back to /pricing, never to any account/billing management screen.
4. Re-ran the grep a second time (verify twice, deterministic) — identical zero-result finding.
expected: A paying/prospective customer should have a self-service way to view their current plan and open the Stripe billing portal (cancel/downgrade/update payment method) from the product, most naturally surfaced on /pricing or from the dashboard paywall's 'manage your account' promise.
observed: No UI entry point anywhere invokes POST /billing/portal or GET /billing/subscription, despite both being implemented and reachable server-side.

summary: The pricing page's checkout error handler only special-cases HTTP 401 (silent — already redirected) and HTTP 503 ('Checkout isn't available yet — billing is still being set up.'). Every other status, including 400 ('no Stripe price configured') and 429 (checkout rate limiter, 5/hr/user), falls through to a generic 'Could not start checkout. Please try again.' The 429 response's Retry-After header is never read or shown to the user, so a rate-limited user has no way to know when to retry.
reproduction: 1. As admin (Free plan), click 'Subscribe to Starter' on /pricing five times within an hour (or reuse the shared account's existing usage).

2. First calls return HTTP 400 {"detail":"This plan is not yet available for purchase (no Stripe price configured)"}; the UI shows only the generic 'Could not start checkout. Please try again.' (test-artifacts/11-auth-starter-checkout-error.png, 12-auth-pro-checkout-error.png).

3. After the 5th checkout attempt in the rolling hour window, subsequent clicks return HTTP 429 {"detail":"Too many checkout attempts, retry later"} with a Retry-After header — the UI still shows the identical generic message (test-artifacts/network-events-step3.json shows two 429s for Starter/Pro; test-artifacts/21-auth-power-checkout-result.png and 22-auth-power-annual-checkout-result.png show the same for Power, both monthly and annual).
4. Confirmed by code inspection of apps/web/src/app/pricing/page.tsx handleSubscribe() — only `err.status === 401` and `err.status === 503` are branched; re-read twice (verify twice, deterministic).
expected: Distinct, honest messaging per failure class — at minimum, a rate-limited checkout attempt should tell the user they're rate-limited and roughly when they can retry (Retry-After).
observed: All non-401/503 failures collapse into one generic message; no fabricated success in any case (no defect in correctness), but the UX doesn't help a legitimately rate-limited or blocked user understand why.

summary: The Free tier's 'Get started free' CTA is a plain <Link href="/signup"> with no auth-state awareness: an already-authenticated user (e.g. admin, already on the Free plan) clicking it lands on a full 'Create account' signup form (name/email/password fields) rather than being told they already have an account or routed to the dashboard.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, navigate to /pricing.
2. Click 'Get started free' (data-testid=subscribe-free).
3. Observe navigation to /signup showing the full create-account form (test-artifacts/13-auth-free-cta-result.png), with no indication the visitor is already signed in.

4. Confirmed by code: apps/web/src/app/pricing/page.tsx renders `<Link href="/signup">` unconditionally for the isFree branch, regardless of session state; re-checked twice (verify twice, deterministic).
expected: An authenticated visitor's 'Get started free' click should ideally recognize the existing session (e.g., redirect to /dashboard, or show 'You're already on the Free plan').
observed: Full signup form rendered with no session awareness — not broken, just not session-aware; low impact since the signup form itself still functions and doesn't lose data.
